cataloging_subject.label: The Chronicle attributed to Robert of Gloucester
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloged unit is one Middle English verse chronicle represented by William Aldis Wright's 1887 two-recension presentation. Wright's longer-recension main stream and forty-eight shorter-recension appendix blocks are representation and version boundaries within that one work, not forty-nine separate works. The unit excludes Wright's corrections and omitted front, glossary, index, and back matter as independent work content; it also keeps the medieval work, eight physical witnesses, Brock's working transcript, Wright's edition volumes, the U-M encoding, and the pinned repository artifact as distinct layers.

catalog_summary.title: The Chronicle attributed to Robert of Gloucester
catalog_summary.author: Robert of Gloucester (attributed; identity and extent of responsibility uncertain)
catalog_summary.date: Both recensions after 1272; shorter recension after 1284; longer recension after 1297 and perhaps after 1307
catalog_summary.region: Middle English work associated linguistically with the Worcestershire–Gloucestershire area; exact place of composition unknown

titles.value: The Chronicle attributed to Robert of Gloucester

assertion.notes: The wording qualifies the traditional attribution while retaining the established discovery title.

titles.value: The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester

assertion.notes: This is the source-edition title, not evidence that Robert's authorship is certain.

titles.value: The Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester
titles.supplied_by: Wright main-text running head as encoded by U-M

agents.name: Robert of Gloucester
notes: Traditional attributed author. The first securely traced attribution is John Stow's 1570 edition, and the surviving medieval evidence does not establish a single author's identity.
Wright allows that the Robert reference may identify only the writer of the long-recension continuation rather than the author of the whole Chronicle.

agents.name: William Aldis Wright
notes: Editor and collator of the 1887 source edition represented by ahb1378; not the medieval author.

agents.name: E. Brock
notes: Prepared the Cotton transcript praised by Wright. Full identity, survival, and present location of the working transcript remain unresolved.

assertion.notes: The conventional name is retained for discovery but not promoted to established sole authorship.

notes: The number and sequence of contributors or revisers behind the two recensions remain uncertain.

scope.description: Wright's 1887 two-volume edition and its two-recension presentation, not composition of the medieval work
Main text, variant notes, shorter-recension appendix blocks, and corrections

assertion.notes: Wright names eight collated sigla. Add. 18631 and the Hunterian witness are expressly excluded from the collated set.

scope.description: Working transcript of Cotton Caligula A XI used in preparation of Wright's edition

assertion.notes: The transcript was a material aid, not an exclusive textual route replacing Wright's direct consultation.

date_statements.display: The shorter recension was composed or revised after 1284

scope.description: The shorter-recension tradition represented through Wright's appendix blocks and Greek-siglum witnesses

assertion.notes: The lower bound is an internal historical argument, not a dated authorial document.

date_statements.display: The longer recension was composed or revised after 1297 and perhaps after 1307

scope.description: The longer-recension main stream represented principally from Cotton Caligula A XI

assertion.notes: The stronger post-1307 proposal remains qualified. It is not silently harmonized with the British Library's last-quarter-thirteenth-century date for Cotton Caligula A XI.

date_statements.display: First printed by Thomas Hearne at Oxford in 1724; Wright's source edition appeared in 1887

assertion.notes: This is the first publication identified in the inspected edition history, not a claim that Wright's edition was the first print appearance.

date_statements.display: Legendary foundation of Britain through thirteenth-century English history; the shorter continuation reaches Edward I's accession in 1272

places.label: Worcestershire–Gloucestershire area
notes: A cautious linguistic region in scholarship, not an asserted writing location or a rigid modern-county boundary.

assertion.notes: Fisher associates the original dialect with the Worcestershire/Gloucestershire area while resisting overprecise localization.

language_statements.label: Middle English
notes: Modern English and Latin occur in Wright's prefatory and corrective apparatus but are not counted as intrinsic languages of the medieval verse work.

form_statements.description: A Middle English historical chronicle in rhyming couplets. The pinned XML represents one verse work through 197 LG elements and 13,683 raw L elements across Wright's longer-recension main stream and shorter-recension appendix presentation; those raw element counts are not canonical lineation.

assertion.notes: The Chronicle compiles and reshapes earlier historical traditions rather than functioning as a neutral event record.

subject_statements.label: Kingship, succession, conquest, and political legitimacy
Henry III, baronial conflict, and the Second Barons' War

summaries.value: A Middle English verse history that begins with the geography and praise of England and the legendary foundation of Britain by Brutus, then narrates British, Roman, Anglo-Saxon, Danish, Norman, Angevin, and thirteenth-century rule. Its later history treats the Norman Conquest, Stephen and Matilda, Henry III, Simon de Montfort, baronial conflict, and the transition to Edward I. The surviving work exists in materially different longer and shorter recensions whose authorship and revision sequence remain uncertain.

Wright prints Cotton Caligula A XI as the principal longer-recension main stream, supplies a 152-line Cotton lacuna after line 4920 from Harley 201, records readings from eight sigla, and places shorter-recension additions and alternatives in forty-eight appendix blocks A–XX. The U-M encoding retains the main stream, appendix, and corrections while representing front matter, preface, glossary/index, and back matter only as omitted placeholders.

The abstract Chronicle is counted once. Wright's main and appendix streams are zero-item representation children beneath that count-bearing work; the forty-eight appendix blocks are not poems or works in their own right. Corrections and omitted-material placeholders remain editorial/digital representation boundaries. Physical witnesses, working documents, edition volumes, scans, the U-M OCR, and the pinned XML are described separately in lineage.

extent.description: One abstract verse Chronicle is counted once. The pinned XML has 61 representation DIV2 sections: 13 in Wright's main stream and 48 appendix blocks. It contains 197 LG and 13,683 raw L elements: 194 LG / 12,203 L in the main stream and 3 LG / 1,480 L in the appendix. The raw L total is an XML representation count, not canonical lineation: starred additions, a locally numbered 152-line Harley supply after line 4920, gaps, and merged printed positions prevent one-to-one equivalence with Wright's conventional line numbers. The 12,637 NOTE1 elements and corrections at printed pp. 1010–1017 are apparatus, not additional works or verse items.

content_parts.label: The Chronicle attributed to Robert of Gloucester
content_parts.summary: The single count-bearing intellectual work represented through Wright's longer- and shorter-recension editorial streams.
notes: The parent carries the single item and form count. Its structural verse metrics are zero because the two zero-item children partition all 197 LG and 13,683 raw L without duplication.

content_parts.label: Wright's longer-recension main stream
content_parts.summary: The main printed text, principally following Cotton Caligula A XI, with a 152-line lacuna after line 4920 supplied from Harley 201 and readings from the other collated witnesses.

locators.value: Chronicle ff. 3r–13v and 15r–168v; two leaves missing after f. 74

notes: Zero item count prevents this recension presentation from duplicating the parent work count.
The raw L count includes the Harley supply and does not equal a canonical line range.

content_parts.label: Wright's shorter-recension appendix presentation
content_parts.summary: Forty-eight editorial blocks A–XX containing shorter-recension additions and alternatives keyed to the main stream.
notes: The forty-eight block labels are apparatus locators and version records, not forty-eight works or poems.
Zero item and item-form counts preserve the single-work boundary while the LG/L metrics report the raw represented verse.

content_parts.label: Wright's corrections apparatus
content_parts.summary: Two encoded correction divisions covering printed pp. 1010–1017, including results of Wright's direct rechecking of Harley readings.
notes: Editorial apparatus, not independent work content.

content_parts.label: Omitted-matter placeholders in the U-M representation
content_parts.summary: Structural placeholders for omitted front matter, preface, glossary and index, and back matter, preserving page sequence without encoding those source-edition sections.

locators.value: DIV1 types omitted front matter, omitted preface, omitted glossary and index, and omitted back matter; 223 placeholder PB

notes: These placeholders are representation boundaries, not additional work content or evidence that the omitted prose is part of the medieval Chronicle.

related_works.title: The Chronicle attributed to Robert of Gloucester (long recension)
notes: CME00022 catalogs the long recension through Hearne's Harley/Cotton hybrid presentation. ahb1378 catalogs the same Chronicle at a broader two-recension boundary through Wright's edition; neither representation should be mistaken for an unrelated medieval work.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME source XML, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/ahb1378.xml.
evidence.summary: Primary local evidence for U-M identifiers, 2006 uncorrected-OCR declaration, Wright 1887 source description, title pages, represented main text, forty-eight appendix blocks, corrections, omitted-matter placeholders, page sequence, checksum, blob, and pinned CME commit.

evidence.citation: Target-scoped structural inspection of pinned ahb1378 XML.
evidence.locator: All DIV1/DIV2, LG, L, NOTE1, P, and PB elements; appendix block labels A–XX; line-number anomaly samples
evidence.summary: Verified 8 DIV1, 61 DIV2, 53 HEAD, 197 LG, 13,683 L, 12,637 NOTE1, 570 P, and 1,112 PB. The main stream has 13 DIV2 / 194 LG / 12,203 L; the appendix has 48 DIV2 / 3 LG / 1,480 L. Inspection also established the 152-line Harley supply, starred insertions, gaps, and merged positions that prevent treating raw L as canonical lineation.

evidence.citation: William Aldis Wright, ed., The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester, Rolls Series 86, 2 vols. (London: printed for Her Majesty's Stationery Office by Eyre and Spottiswoode, 1887), complete Internet Archive scans.
evidence.locator: vol. I preface and pp. 1–494; vol. II pp. 495–877 and corrections pp. 1010–1017
evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence for title, editor, imprint, the two-volume expression, main-text and appendix boundaries, page lineation, notes, and corrections. Both complete PDFs were cached and inspected.

evidence.citation: William Aldis Wright, Preface, The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester, vol. I (1887), pp. ix–l.
evidence.locator: vol. I preliminary pp. ix–l; especially recension analysis, manuscript descriptions, source method, Brock acknowledgement, and List of MSS. used
evidence.summary: Wright's direct account of the two recensions, uncertain Robert attribution, Cotton base text, Harley lacuna supply and rechecking, Brock transcript, eight collated sigla, excluded late witnesses, and appendix method.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge Archives, Add. MS c/172, Collation of the manuscripts of Robert of Gloucester, August 1868.
evidence.summary: Direct process evidence for Wright's collation of Cotton Caligula A XI and Bodleian Digby 205, with readings from Trinity R.4.26 and Cambridge University Library Ee.4.31. It is not treated as a textual ancestor.

evidence.citation: Matthew Fisher, Once Called Albion: The Composition and Transmission of History Writing in England, 1280–1350 (DPhil thesis, University of Oxford, 2005), chapters 3–4.
evidence.summary: Specialist evidence for the uncertain attribution, fifteen-manuscript and two-recension model, preferable longer/shorter terminology, composition-date bounds, linguistic region, witness identities, and unresolved revision sequence. Complete thesis PDF cached.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Caligula A XI component, record 041-001102341.
evidence.summary: Current repository evidence for the imperfect Chronicle at ff. 3r–13v and 15r–168v, last-quarter-thirteenth-century date, and physical/custodial context.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 201, record 040-002046029.
evidence.summary: Current repository evidence for the Chronicle at ff. 4r–161r, 1400–1424 date, and Hearne/Wright source role.

evidence.citation: General Corpus descriptive and lineage manifests for CME00022.
evidence.locator: cataloging_subject, date statements, related source layers, and evidence
evidence.summary: Repository evidence for the separately cataloged long-recension unit represented by Hearne and Bagster, used to keep the intellectual-work relationship explicit without collapsing edition boundaries.

open_questions.question: Who composed and revised the two recensions, and how should the internally argued post-1297 or post-1307 long-recension date be reconciled with Cotton Caligula A XI's current last-quarter-thirteenth-century catalog date?
next_steps: Review post-2005 scholarship on the Robert self-reference, recension chronology, and manuscript stemma.
Seek the British Library's current codicological basis for the Cotton component date.
Retain separate attributed claims rather than forcing one normalized date or one-author model.

open_questions.question: Does E. Brock's working transcript of Cotton Caligula A XI survive, and where is it now held?
next_steps: Search Wright's surviving papers and Trinity College archival descriptions for Brock's transcript.
Ask Trinity and the British Library whether a transcript matching Wright's acknowledgement is identifiable.
Keep the transcript as a documented process layer with unknown survival rather than inventing a present holding.

open_questions.question: Which physical or scanned copy underlies U-M's OCR, what correction and page-image history applies, and what item-specific terms govern the XML markup?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for item-level production metadata, source-copy provenance, and any preserved page-image package.
Distinguish the XML header's historical linked-image statement from currently verified downloadable image access.
Retain current No Copyright–United States treatment of historical materials without inferring an open markup license.

notes: Counting axis: one intellectual work; sixty-one XML representation sections; forty-eight appendix apparatus blocks; zero additional works from recensions, appendix blocks, corrections, or omitted placeholders.
The δ siglum is Senate House Library MS 278, formerly Lord Mostyn Library no. 259; the old and current shelfmarks are one physical witness, not two.
All physical, editorial, and digital source layers and their rights/access states are delegated to lineage:ahb1378.
